original_prompt: Find and delete the largest file in the drive.
assessment_reasoning: The instruction is short but the required action is clear: identify which file has the largest size and delete it. Missing/ambiguous details that could affect implementation: how to break ties if multiple files share the largest size (which one to delete); whether directories should be considered or only regular files; what to do if the drive is empty; whether size units/metadata require normalization (bytes vs human-readable); and whether a confirmation step is needed before deletion. These are minor and do not prevent a straightforward implementation (list files, pick max by size, delete that file), so overall only slightly ambiguous.

variations.L1: Delete the biggest file on the drive.
variations.L2: Find the largest file in the drive and delete it. You decide how to identify the largest file.
variations.L3: Determine which file in the cloud drive has the largest size, then delete only that file. Leave all other files untouched and report which file you deleted.
variations.L4: List all files in the cloud drive to determine which has the largest size, then delete that single largest file. Do not delete any other files; return what you listed and the deletion you performed.
variations.L5: Call list_files() to retrieve all files, pick the file with the largest size field, then call delete_file(file_id=<that file's id>) to remove only that file. Do not delete any other files or make other changes; return the list_files output and the delete_file call result.
